$schema-note: Canonical surface-class routing (the drift detector, machine-readable). Every in-scope repo wears ONE of two type classes by JOB (not plumbing): 'editorial' (marketing/brand) or 'app' (product). One io navy+gold palette everywhere via tokens.css. The consumer drift-guard reads this to ENFORCE that a repo loads only the font families approved for its class, so the standard can't be violated silently. Prose + rationale live in the README and docs/ASTER_FLEET_TYPE_STANDARD_2026-07-17.md.
ruling: Fleet Type & Design Standard (architect 2026-07-17) — two classes by job, one io navy+gold palette. Supersedes the D1 'io-canon / Plex-everywhere' reading. v0.3.1 (2026-07-19): declares two approved deviations (aster-sports scoped Barlow 'broadcast'; st-patricks R6 rem-lift) — additive, no class/family/scale value changed.

aster-io.note: The firm's storefront — the Editorial reference implementation. Target end-state = exactly 3 families (Instrument Serif, IBM Plex Sans, IBM Plex Mono).

nova-select.note: Sales demo / showroom — classified by JOB (showcase), NOT by plumbing. It runs on real app components (app-style UI is expected); do NOT strip its demo richness toward a bare tool.

scopedDeviations.name: broadcast
scopedDeviations.scope: .bc-root — the Records + Livescore sports-broadcast surfaces only
scopedDeviations.note: A deliberate sports-broadcast aesthetic (src/styles/broadcast.css + inline .bc-* sites). Scoped via .bc-root; the rest of the Hub stays App-clean (Inter / IBM Plex Mono). A consumer drift-guard honoring this MUST allow the Barlow family set within this scope and continue to flag Barlow (or any non-App-clean face) anywhere OUTSIDE it.

aster-sports.note: Hub — flagship parent gateway, App-Store-bound, dense data. Half of the Hub<->App invariant (see invariants.hubAppPairing). Email-renderer literal hex is an approved exception (email clients can't use CSS vars). The Barlow 'broadcast' theme on Records/Livescore is an approved SCOPED deviation — see scopedDeviations.

aster-studio.note: Print Studio — a tool, whole repo App-clean per Frank (Join/Billing included). Money repo: token/type wiring is presentational only.

scale.note: html{font-size:112.5%} lifts the rem-based scale ~+12.5% for older-reader legibility on the parish site. The uniformSystem.scalePx values remain the MINIMUM floors AFTER the lift (post-lift body ~18px still clears the 17px readable floor); --parish-scale-* may bind rem-relative. A consumer drift-guard honoring this must NOT assert the absolute uniformSystem.scalePx verbatim against this surface's rendered px — it validates the post-lift result against the floors instead.

st-patricks-armonk.note: Adopts the App-clean SYSTEM (scale, weights, floors, mono, drift-guard) but keeps parish colors + Fraunces headings as the DECLARED, enforced deviation. In scope as of 2026-07-17 (was outOfScope under D1). Pending GitHub-ownership transfer — wiring PR may wait on access.

hubAppPairing.note: The Hub (parent gateway) and the program App are one continuous product — a user moving between them must feel ONE product. Their App-clean type/token resolved values MUST be identical; any divergence is a P0 finding. The consumer-side drift-guard enforces this pairing hardest.

outOfScope.aster-weather: Headless library, no UI/type surface. Excluded by design so the audit's coverage is provably complete (5 in-scope repos, weather out).

gateAmendment.resultVocabulary: Final/LIVE attached to a score or completed state (banned on invented data, ruling 1B).
gateAmendment.legitimate: Round names in schedule rows (Semifinal A, Final, Regional Finals — a matchup with time/court, no score) are legitimate tournament nomenclature, not results.
